entry.title: How to Learn System Design with Jordan Has No Life

group.title: How to Learn System Design with Jordan Has No Life
group.description:
Stefan interviews Jordan, the creator of the YouTube channel 'Jordan Has No Life.' They discuss Jordan's journey into system design, his learning process, and the evolution of his content creation. Jordan shares insights on the importance of understanding core concepts, the value of personal projects, and the trends in system design. They also touch on the impact of AI on engineering and the significance of practical decision-making in system design.

Jordan's channel: @jordanhasnolife5163 
Learn System Design: https://www.hellointerview.com/learn/system-design
Schedule mock interviews with real interviewers at top companies: https://www.hellointerview.com

00:00 Introduction to Jordan and His Journey
01:00 The Birth of a YouTube Channel: Learning Systems Design
03:35 Learning Process: From Books to Teaching
07:25 Transitioning to Deep Dives: A Shift in Focus
09:49 Advice for Newcomers: Building a Strong Foundation
12:57 Personal Projects: Practical Learning Experiences
15:44 Trends in System Design: The Evolution of Technologies
18:01 Emerging Technologies in Data Engineering
20:13 The Evolution of Databases and Their Applications
23:53 Innovative Systems and Technologies
26:18 Career Insights and Real-World Applications
31:09 The Role of AI in System Design

entry.title: Networking Essentials for System Design Interviews

group.title: Networking Essentials for System Design Interviews
group.description:
We'll cover the important topics of networking you're likely to encounter in system design interviews: OSI Model, IP, TCP/UDP, HTTP, SSE, Websockets, gRPC, GraphQL,  WebRTC, Load Balancing, Regionalization, Retries with Backoff and Jitter, Circuit Breakers.

00:00 Introduction
01:10 OSI Model
02:47 HTTP Request Breakdown
04:22 Internet Protocol (IP)
07:38 TCP/UDP
12:37 Hypertext Transport Protocol (HTTP)
15:01 Representational State Transfer (REST)
18:36 GraphQL
23:00 Google Remote Procedure Call (gRPC)
26:42 Server Sent Events (SSE)
30:34 WebSockets (WS)
34:18 WebRTC (Real-time Communication)
38:00 Horizontal and Vertical Scaling
39:35 Load Balancing
40:00 Client-Side Load Balancing
43:00 Dedicated Load Balancers
46:06 Layer 4 and Layer 7 Load Balancers
50:03 Regionalization
55:49 Timeouts, Backoff, and Retries
1:00:15 Cascading Failures and Circuit Breakers
1:04:35 Summary

Companion Writeup: https://www.hellointerview.com/learn/system-design/core-concepts/networking-essentials
Realtime Updates: https://www.hellointerview.com/learn/system-design/deep-dives/realtime-updates

group.title: Behavioral Interview Discussion with Ex-Meta Hiring Committee Member
group.description:
In this conversation, Stefan Mai interviews Austen McDonald, a former senior engineering manager and hiring committee member at Meta, about the how behavioral interviews are assessed, what you should do to prepare, and common red flags. 

Check out Austen's substack here: https://thebehavioral.substack.com/
For preparing behavioral anecdotes with AI, use Story Builder for free: https://www.hellointerview.com/learn/behavioral/story-builder

entry.title: SQL vs NoSQL is the WRONG Question (System Design Tips)

group.title: SQL vs NoSQL is the WRONG Question (System Design Tips)
group.description:
Stop asking "SQL or NoSQL?" in your system design interviews! 🚫 

Learn the three crucial factors you should focus on instead:
1️⃣ Data Model
2️⃣ Consistency Requirements
3️⃣ Query Patterns

Real examples using Postgres & DynamoDB included! Perfect for software engineers preparing for tech interviews.

#SystemDesign #TechInterview #SoftwareEngineering #DatabaseDesign #Shorts #CodingInterview #PostgreSQL #DynamoDB #TechTips #Programming
